text:
Market today: Modest risk-on tone. Meta's cloud announcement is the real driver - it's pulling cloud/AI infrastructure names, but simultaneously hammering CoreWeave and Nebius as new competition. Chips are mixed; no macro shock today, just stock-specific rotation inside tech.

Your holdings: SOXL (-10.26%) is your loudest problem - 3x leveraged, decaying daily, sitting only ~14% above your $200 stop. On margin, a bad chip session accelerates the pain fast. SPAL is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO down -13.12% with your stop just 2% below current price at $21.38 - that's basically a hair trigger, one bad tick and you're out. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec bleeding out; no catalyst in today's data to justify holding pain here.

Watch: (1) June jobs report coming - a hot number crushes rate-cut hopes and hits your entire tech/chip book hard. (2) CoreWeave/cloud infrastructure names - Meta's cloud move is live and repricing the space now.

Bottom line: Your leveraged positions are the real risk - protect them before adding anything new.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - if chips soften today, consider trimming to reduce leveraged decay drag; don't wait for the stop to save you on margin.
SPAL: Stop at $21.38 is razor-thin. Decide now if you want to widen it intentionally or let it run off - sitting passive on a fast-moving leveraged ETF is the worst option.
No new buys today - AMD and TSM have clean scanner setups but no fresh catalyst from today's headlines to justify adding chip exposure when SOXL is already bleeding.
